role: Former SNSC Secretary (2013–2023); former Defense Minister (1997–2005)
bio: Ali Shamkhani is an ethnic Arab from Khuzestan who rose through the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) Navy to become one of Iran's most senior security officials. He commanded the IRGC Navy during the Iran–Iraq War and later served as combined IRGC/Regular Navy commander. [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami) appointed him Defense Minister in 1997 — a moderate appointment widely seen as signalling detente — and he served for the full eight years. [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) appointed him Secretary of the [Supreme National Security Council](/orgs/supreme-national-security-council) in 2013, where he served until 2023. He brokered the 2023 China-mediated Iran–Saudi Arabia normalisation agreement. He was replaced as SNSC Secretary by Ali Akbar Ahmadian in 2023.
editorial_notes: Born 1955 in Ahvaz. IRGC Navy commander. Defense Minister 1997–2005. SNSC Secretary 2013–2023. Brokered China-mediated Iran–Saudi normalisation in March 2023.
